data.description: UK political dysfunction is driving investors away. With years of instability and better alternatives elsewhere, capital is moving out, and risk premia are starting to reappear, particularly in bonds.

data.title: Logan Paul sells rare Pokémon card for $16.5M after NFT controversy, with investors suing over lost returns from Liquid Marketplace.
data.description: In 2022, Paul split ownership of a valuable Pokémon card on Liquid Marketplace before the site shut down. This decision triggered a legal battle as investors sought refunds for their shares.

data.title: Bitcoin remains flat at $68,886.53, Ethereum gains 1.57% at $2,001.73, XRP sees a 0.76% increase at $1.48, while Dogecoin slides -1.54% at $0.1007. Market volatility noted with declines & gains as analyst suggests accumulation in crypto during downtimes.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies traded flat on Monday due to thin liquidity from the holiday. Bitcoin increased by 0.11% to $68,886.53, while Ethereum rose by 1.57% to $2,001.73. XRP and Solana also saw minor gains of 0.76%. Dogecoin, however, fell by 1.54% to $0.1007. Bitcoin hit an intraday high of $70,060 before dropping back to the $68,000 range with a trading volume decrease of 17%. Ethereum reached $2,000 as its trading volume fell by 40%. Bitcoin's market dominance reduced to 58.3%, and Ethereum's share contracted to 10.2%. Over $215 million was liquidated in the past 24 hours, with long liquidations causing most losses. Bitcoin's open interest fell by 0.08%, while Ethereum's locked funds in derivatives increased by 0.88%. The market sentiment was characterized by "Extreme Fear."
Mobil to face A$16 million in fines for misleading fuel claims at nine stations, according to Australia’s ACCC.
Australia’s ACCC says Mobil cooperated, admitted liability, and agreed to make joint submissions with the ACCC to the court.
The yield on the 20-year JGB eased to 3.03%, down 5 basis points.

data.title: Mazda Foundation grants $586,361 in 2026 to 9 non-profits for food security, education, and workforce development.
data.description: In Irvine, California on February 16, 2026, the Mazda Foundation (USA), Inc. disclosed $586,361 in grant funding for 2026. The funding aims to aid nine nonprofit partners engaged in vital initiatives related to food security, education, STEM, and workforce development in the regions serviced by Mazda.

data.title: Faraday Future CEO YT Jia addresses market performance, plans for improvement in EAI Robotics and recommendation for AIxC.
data.description: LOS ANGELES, Feb 4 (BUSINESS WIRE) - Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc., trading as FFAI on NASDAQ, provided a business update by YT Jia, the Founder and Global Co-CEO. The California-headquartered company is a leader in shared intelligent electric mobility solutions worldwide. YT Jia expressed greetings for Chinese New Year and emphasized the importance of consistency. He highlighted significant developments over the past year, showcasing tangible progress.
Analysts warn that China’s surge in AI could challenge UStech dominance, potentially triggering a global tech shock-cnbc
